description: Magic that reshapes reality — transforming bodies, transmuting matter, reversing time's work on objects, and bending physical form.
abilities: Feign Death

Placing several nearby willing creatures into suspended animation for up to an hour. Those affected cannot see, feel, speak, or move, though they remain dimly aware of sounds around them. Their bodies grow cold and pulseless, perfectly mimicking death to all but magical inspection. Harm or conscious choice ends the effect immediately.
Genesis Command

Touching a creature and reversing or accelerating its development to any stage of life. An adult may be reverted to a child, an elder returned to youth, or a young creature aged to maturity. The creature retains all memories unless reverted to infancy, and gains the physical capabilities appropriate for its new stage. Only works against powerful enemies if they are at death's door.
Loosen

Twirling a finger as if unwinding a spool of yarn, starting with a single visible creature nearby and cascading a wave of magical mischief outward. Anything fastened or tightened around nearby creatures comes undone: bags unclasp and spill their contents, laces untie, belts unbuckle, shirts unbutton, and leashed animals break free.
Petrify

Turning the surface of a nearby lesser foe to stone for a brief duration, leaving the target unable to move, see, hear, or speak. The petrified creature becomes durable but brittle -- if enough damage shatters the stone shell, the creature is destroyed. Alternatively, the caster may encase their own armor or clothing in a chitinous shell that absorbs incoming damage; a nonmagical weapon that strikes the carapace shatters on impact. The shell crumbles after absorbing a blow, and it can be summoned reflexively in response to an incoming attack.
Pinch

Imbuing your fingers with supernatural strength for a short time. While active, you can effortlessly pick up and move objects of any weight, so long as you can reasonably handle their size and shape — pinching a bully's shirt to hoist them into the air, or casually sliding a giant anvil across the floor.
Reshape

Sculpting a creature's facial features through touch, making them appear older, younger, or like an entirely different person. Replicating a specific face requires a visible reference during the casting. The transformation is permanent and can only be undone by restorative magic or a second application of this ability.
Steelsprout

Extending a hand and closing the eyes to permanently transform a nearby nonmagical metal object -- no larger than a door -- into a delicate weave of living plants. The caster envisions the species and shape of the plants that emerge.
Strech

Instantly enlarging or shrinking a nearby creature or object. At basic power, anything from ant-sized to horse-sized can grow up to twice its size or shrink to half, with weight changing proportionally. With greater effort, the effect extends to targets as large as an elephant. The target cannot be enlarged beyond the confines of its surrounding space.
The Bitter Gift

Bestowing the spark of self-awareness upon a creature that lacks it. Through a spoken meditation on the meaning of life, the target falls unconscious as its body and mind undergo a rapid evolution toward sentience. It awakens transformed: capable of language, possessed of identity, and haunted by dim memories of its former existence. It knows who gave it the gift of personhood.
To Dust
Returning a crafted object to nature, reducing it instantly and permanently to dust. The object must fit within a modest space. The transformation cannot be reversed.
Undo

Speaking a word of power to reverse the fate of a nearby object, unwinding the effort that shaped it. The spell affects only nonliving things and only the discrete results of another creature's work — unlacing shoes, unthreading a garment, collapsing furniture into raw lumber, or reverting a sculpture to a block of marble. The more effort invested, the further back the reversal reaches — from moments of work undone at its lightest, to days, weeks, or any amount of effort erased entirely at full power.
